ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF COURT ORDER DATED 10/5/2016 ACCEPTING DEFENDANTS' 2-PAGE DISCOVERY DISCLOSURE AND STAYING CASE AS A CONSEQUENCE

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM MICHELS' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE OCTOBER 5, 2016 ORDER IS DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT IT IS APPROPRIATE TO DECIDE THAT DISPUTE AT THIS POINT AND INSTEAD THE PARTIES SHOULD PROCEED TO LITIGATE DR. MICHELS' WORKERS' COMPENSATION CASE AND THIS CASE SHOULD REMAIN STAYED UNTIL THE COMPLETION OF THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION CASE. RESOLUTION OF A DISCOVERY DISPUTE IS INCONSISTENT WITH THE JUDICIAL ECONOMY REASON THAT A STAY WAS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: KELLEY ANN DEYOUNG, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT HELD ON APR-15-2015, IN DEPT. 302. THE DEMURRERS OF THE REGENTS AND DR. BICKLER ARE SUSTAINED AS TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE UNTIL 6/01/15 TO AMEND. AS CURRENTLY PLED, ALL THREE OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION ACT AND THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT ULTIMATE FACTS TO OBTAIN RELIEF AGAINST THE REGENTS UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 835 OR DR. BICKLER UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 840.2. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE (UNTIL 6/01/15) TO AMEND, IF HE IS ABLE TO DO, TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT HIS CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY PROVISIONS OF THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION ACT AND THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF AGAINST THE REGENTS UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 835 AND DR. BICKLER UNDER GOVERNMENT CODE 840.2. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

Declaration And Motion For Reconsideration Of Court Order Dated 10/5/2016 Accepting Defendants 2-Page Discovery Disclosure And Staying Case As A Consequence; Reconsideration Moiton Urging Requirement That Defendants Be Compelled To Reply To Discovery Questions Enclosed
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Plaintiff William Michels' motion for reconsideration of the October 5, 2016 order is denied. Dr. Michels has not presented new or different law, facts or circumstances warranting reconsideration of the prior order nor, even were the court to reconsider the prior order, has Dr. Michels shown that any different ruling should be made. As a courtesy to Dr. Michels, the court required that the defendants provide a list of viruses before the stay in this case would go in effect. Defendants provided the list. Dr. Michels' motion is, in effect, a discovery motion arguing that the list provided by defendants was insufficient. The court does not believe that it is appropriate to decide that dispute at this point and instead the parties should proceed to litigate Dr. Michels' workers' compensation case and this case should remain stayed until the completion of the workers' compensation case. Resolution of a discovery dispute is inconsistent with the judicial economy reason that a stay was granted. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Ann DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - WILLIAM J. MICHAELS, TEL. NO. 1-415-524-2816 PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. ALEXEI N. OFFILL-KLEIN OF THE LOMBARDI LOPER & CONANT, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-510-433-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANT DR PHILIP BICKLER MD'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Dr. Philip Bickler's demurrer to all three causes of action in the second amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for negligence and sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Liberally construed, the first cause of action adequately alleges facts supporting a negligence claim grounded in Government Code 820(a) based on improper handling and control of virus material which led to Dr. Michels' exposure to that material. The face of the second amended complaint does not necessarily show that the first cause of action is barred by the immunities in Government Code 820.2 or 855.4. The second cause of action insufficiently alleges a contract that covers exposure to the virus material and it does not appear that there is a reasonable possibility that Dr. Michaels can allege such a contract. The third cause of action is wholly duplicative of the first cause of action. The case is stayed pursuant to the agreement of the parties pending the resolution of the workers compensation case. Parties to meet and confer as to form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: William Michaels telephone number 415-524-2816 Plaintiff in Propria Persona.
GayLynn Conant of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP telephone number 510-433-2600 for Defendants The University of California and Dr. Bickler.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modifed its tenative ruling.
DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend in part and sustained with leave to amend in part. Plaintiff has pled facts showing that the complaint is not barred by the exclusive remedy provisions in the Workers' Compensation Act. Plaintiff alleges that he returned to the campus in in July 2012, after his employment ended. (FAC paragraphs 5-7.) On a demurrer, the court must accept the facts pled in the complaint as true. The 2012, visit does not fall under the employee definition provided in Labor Code section 3351. The demurrer to the second cause of action for premise liability, as alleged against Defendant Regents of the University of California and Defendant Philip Bickler, is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has not pled facts demonstrating that Defendant Regents of the University of California is liable under Government Code section 835 or that Defendant Philip Bickler is liable under Government Code section 840.2. The demurrer to the first, third, and fourth causes of action, as alleged against Defendant Regents of the University of California, is sustained, with thirty days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege the statutory basis for the claims. (Government Code section 815.) The demurrer to the first and third causes of actions as alleged against Defendant Regents of the University of California and Defendant Philip Bickler is sustained, with thirty days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege facts that Defendants breached a duty owed to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has failed to identify a specific act on the part of either Defendant that supports a claim of negligence. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action, as alleged against Defendant Regents of the University of California and Defendant Philip Bickler is sustained, with thirty days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege facts that defendants engaged in extreme or outrageous conduct. The cause of action asserts conclusory allegations that Defendants' acts were intentional, willful, wanton, malicious, and oppressive. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Atty. GAYLYNN KIRN CONANT of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP, Tel No. (510) 433-2600 appearing for Defendants Regents of the University of California and Dr. Philip Bickler MD.

RULING - The Court made disclosure to parties regarding friendship with General Counsel of the University of California, and that this friendship would not prevent the Court from being fair or impartial in this matter. The matter was argued and submitted by parties. The Court adopted its tentative ruling (with the sole modification of allowing until 6/1/15 to amend): As to DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, the demurrers of the Regents and Dr. Bickler are sustained as to all three causes of action. Plaintiff is given leave until 6/1/15 to amend. As currently pled, all three of the causes of action are barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and the first cause of action for premises liability fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to obtain relief against the Regents under Government Code section 835 or Dr. Bickler under Government Code 840.2. Plaintiff is granted leave (until 6/1/15) to amend, if he is able to do, to allege ultimate facts showing that his claims are not barred by the exclusive remedy provisions of the Workers' Compensation Act and that he is entitled to relief against the Regents under Government Code section 835 and Dr. Bickler under Government Code 840.2. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)
